votes.reason: The Python code correctly checks that the result is None when the text is empty, and not None when the text is non-empty, matching the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: when text is empty, it checks that result is None; when text is non-empty, it checks that result is not None (i.e., Some hash exists). The conjunction in Coq with implications means exactly this behavior on concrete inputs.

It enforces: result is None iff text is empty; otherwise result is not None, matching the Coq spec’s two implications.
